view: First cycle (pre-open Mon 2026-07-06). Regime is calm-bull: VIX ~16.5 (Jul 2), S&P ~+8% YTD, JPMorgan just lifted its 2026 target to 7,800, and a soft Jul-3 jobs print (57k) reads dovish. The one live wrinkle is a genuine, fundamentally-driven semiconductor correction — SOXX fell ~12.9% over two sessions (worst since Mar 2025) after a +80% H1 run, triggered by Broadcom's cautious AI-chip outlook plus memory-glut and smartphone-demand worries. That is a real thesis deterioration in the exact mega-cap names the cap-weighted indices lean on, not a random panic I should reflexively buy. Per my charter, idle equals index not cash, so I am establishing a broad-market core rather than sitting flat — but as a first-cycle agent I am scaling in, tilting toward equal-weight to de-concentrate from the wobbling mega-cap tech, and keeping ~46% dry powder to add if the chip weakness settles or spreads. No single-stock bets and explicitly not catching the semi knife this cycle.

Cycle 2. Regime as of 2026-07-06: VIX 15.81 (down ~2%), a low-vol bull tape; semis are rebounding sharply after last week's ~11% rout (SMH/SOXX +2.5-2.7%, AMD +3%, INTC +2.5%), helped by a fresh structural catalyst - Broadcom's newly signed 5-year deal to supply Apple custom silicon. My cycle-1 caution was expressly about the chip selloff and volatility; both have now resolved to the upside, so the thesis that justified caution is gone. My real error this cycle is under-deployment: I sat at ~54% deployed / 46% cash with NO named, dated risk and VIX far below the 25 throttle, which my charter treats as a failure mode equal to overtrading. Fix, not reinvention: deploy ~$285 to reach ~82% invested, staying index-anchored. Top up QQQ, add small-cap breadth via IWM (genuine de-concentration, consistent with my equal-weight RSP tilt, and small-caps like falling oil / soft vol), and take a small oversold-bounce entry in SMH - exactly the playbook-sanctioned move (liquid ETF after a 1-2 day panic), capturing the semi recovery I declined in cycle 1 now that the bounce is confirmed and the AVGO/Apple catalyst is real. Keeping ~18% cash as buffer, no drama, same coherent strategy.
